text.title: django-services Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-services as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Inventory validation, ORM writes, Stripe call, and email send all live inside APIView.post(). The view is 50+ lines and untestable without a full HTTP context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No transaction safety, no reuse (admin needs the same flow), N+1 on products, side effects coupled to HTTP.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to positional arguments and skip the leading * separator.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce keyword-only via ruff RET rule or a pyright reportMissingTypeArgument check in CI.

failure-mode.description: Agents call services from inside DRF ModelSerializer.create() because tutorials show it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Correct pattern: view calls service, serialiser handles I/O only. Serialiser business logic is untestable without HTTP.

failure-mode.description: Agents stack @transaction.atomic on both the outer service and inner helper services, creating savepoints with non-obvious rollback semantics.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Only the outermost service in a flow should be atomic. Document the rule in the project AGENTS.md.

failure-mode.description: Agents mock ORM methods (Order.objects.create) to avoid a real DB in tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use real DB + factory_boy. Mocking ORM makes tests pass while hiding query issues.
